body.title: CLOSED: wire export_pattern/import_pattern for cross-Igor pattern sharing
body.result: export_pattern + import_pattern tools registered; 5 tests pass round-trip, no-clobber, missing-root; 4420 total pass
body.description:
Igor instances should be able to share learned patterns (not data) with each other. T-engram-portability already shipped export_engram() + import_engram() + BFS subgraph extraction + dependency manifest. This ticket wires those into a usable sharing surface: one Igor exports a pattern subtree; another imports it and integrates it into their own memory graph. The scope is light — the infrastructure exists.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/engram_portable.py, wild_igor/igor/tools/ (new share_pattern tool or extend existing), tests/test_engram_portable.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite, test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** IN: export/import of pattern subgraphs across Igor instances. OUT: data sharing, shared belief state, any memory merge that overwrites existing nodes.
**Test plan:** test that export→import round-trip preserves node count + edge structure; test that import into a non-empty graph doesn't clobber existing nodes.
**Pass condition:** Igor A exports a learned pattern subtree; Igor B imports it and can retrieve the pattern nodes via cortex.search.
